mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in housing units sold (HOUST) signals a strengthening demand for durable goods, which boosts consumer confidence and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with rising HOUST are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or HOUST fails to rise for 3 consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in industrial production (INDPRO) signals a weakening supply chain, which reduces production costs and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in periods with falling INDPRO are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or INDPRO fails to decline for 3 consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) signals a tightening monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns in periods with rising FEDFUNDS are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or FEDFUNDS fails to rise above 4.5% for 3 consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in initial unemployment claims (UNRATE) signals a strengthening labor market, which boosts consumer confidence and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with rising UNRATE are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or UNRATE fails to rise for 3 consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in real GDP (GDPC1) signals a strengthening demand for durable goods, which boosts consumer confidence and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in periods with rising GDPC1 are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or GDPC1 fails to rise for 3 consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in industrial production (INDPRO) and a 3-month increase in housing units sold (HOUST) signals a weakening supply chain and a strengthening demand for durable goods, which reduces production costs and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with falling INDPRO and rising HOUST are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or INDPRO fails to decline and HOUST fails to rise for 3 consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) and a 3-month decline in the Consumer Price Index (CPIAUCSL) signals a tightening monetary cycle and a weakening inflationary pressure, which compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS crosses above 4.5% and CPIAUCSL falls below 280
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns in periods with rising FEDFUNDS and falling CPIAUCSL are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or FEDFUNDS fails to rise above 4.5% and CPIAUCSL fails to fall below 280 for 3 consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in initial unemployment claims (UNRATE) and a 3-month decline in payroll employment (PAYEMS) signals a strengthening labor market and a weakening employment growth, which boosts consumer confidence and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with rising UNRATE and falling PAYEMS are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or UNRATE fails to rise and PAYEMS fails to decline for 3 consecutive months.
